body: We wish to be able to use MPI to parallelize matrix multiplication for remapping. This is an initial implementation which allows for remapping from a serial source space to a distributed target space. Note: This implementation only works for remappings where the partitions of the source and target spaces are collocated. For non-aligned meshes, we'll need to implement the super-halo exchange for source data to get correct results. This PR is a part of Coupler SDI [#188](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/issues/188). ## Main changes introduced by this PR * `remap!` supports remapping from serial to distributed spaces, whereas before it only allowed serial to serial remapping. * The `LinearMap` struct is extended to include a field `target_global_elem_lidx`, a mapping from global to local indices on the distributed target space. * When constructing a `LinearMap` object in `generate_map`, the weight data is computed only on the root process (using TempestRemap), then is broadcast to all processes. * The source data is generated on the serial space and broadcast to all processes. ## Main changes needed for the next implementation * `LinearMap` will need a `source_global_elem_lidx` field, which will map global to local indices on the distributed source space. `remap!` will need to change its indexing of `source_array` to use this distributed indexing, similar to the changes we've already made to `target_array` indexing. * `generate_map` will need to remove its dependence on serial spaces. This change will include: * Update calls to `write_exodus` to use distributed spaces (see related PR #1117). * Calculate `unique_nodes` for a distributed space so that we can look up the `source_unique_idxs` and `target_unique_idxs`, which are needed to construct the `LinearMap`. We expect this and the super-halo implementation described below to be the most involved components of the next implementation. * Once the above changes are made, we can use only distributed spaces and make efficient use of MPI, which is ideal. * Generate the source data on a distributed field, and exchange information between processes using the super-halo exchange rather than broadcasting. At first, we can send the entire source data to all processes, then implement and compare the super-halo approach. ## Quality assurance Distribued remapping is compared against serial remapping (i.e. the previous implementation), as well as the analytical solution. The solutions are quantitatively found to have error of less than 10^-20, and are also compared visually in the following plots: - [x] Code follows the [style guidelines](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/) OR N/A. - [x] Unit tests are included OR N/A. - [x] Code is exercised in an integration test OR N/A. - [x] Documentation has been added/updated OR N/A.
title: Distributed regridding v1
